title: #eng-security: 1737839002
preview: Marcus Lin: Kicking off review thread for envelope encryption in Private backup/restore (ENG-9823 / PR-505). Need to get aligned on: (1) threat model assumptions, (2) key rotation expectations, (3) KMS downtime behavior, and (4) what we require for security sign-off + docs. @Hanae Suzuki can you drop current design ...
text:
Marcus Lin: Kicking off review thread for envelope encryption in Private backup/restore (ENG-9823 / PR-505). Need to get aligned on: (1) threat model assumptions, (2) key rotation expectations, (3) KMS downtime behavior, and (4) what we require for security sign-off + docs. @Hanae Suzuki can you drop current design summary here?

Hanae Suzuki: yep. current plan:
- Data key (DEK): randomly generated per backup artifact. Used w/ AES-256-GCM to encrypt `backup.tar`.
- Key-encryption key (KEK): customer-managed KMS key (AWS KMS / customer HSM later), used for envelope: KMS Encrypt(DEK) -> EDEK.
- Artifact layout: `manifest.json` (plaintext) + `backup.tar.enc` + `edek.bin` + `checksums.txt`.
- manifest includes `backup_format_version`, `kms_provider`, `key_id` (ARN), `edek_sha256`, and ciphertext metadata (nonce, gcm tag) but NOT plaintext secrets.
- restore flow: read manifest -> call KMS...

title: #eng-security: 1737395528
preview:
Marcus Lin: Kicking off threat model review for SIEM audit exporter (ENG-5189). Focus areas: secrets handling (Splunk HEC token, Datadog API key, S3 assume-role), egress/SSRF controls, and log redaction. Can folks weigh in on current plan + gaps?

Ethan Park: yup. Control plane side: export config API stores only se...
text:
Marcus Lin: Kicking off threat model review for SIEM audit exporter (ENG-5189). Focus areas: secrets handling (Splunk HEC token, Datadog API key, S3 assume-role), egress/SSRF controls, and log redaction. Can folks weigh in on current plan + gaps?

Ethan Park: yup. Control plane side: export config API stores only secret *references* (vault path / secret id), not raw tokens. Exporter pulls via secrets service at runtime. But we should confirm we never echo secret refs back to non-admin roles.

Kevin Osei: +1. Also want clarity on what we log when adapter fails auth. We can’t dump request headers/body into logs (risk: HEC token / DD key). Need explicit “no sensitive headers in logs” requirement.

Hanae Suzuki: secrets: please ensure we’re using the standard secrets broker (kms envelope, rotation support). Exporter should get a short-lived session token for secret retrieval, not long-liv...

title: #eng-security: 1737059014
preview:
Hanae Suzuki: seeing a couple pings from Support + SE: Private customers not seeing RBAC change events (role updates, user added to project). Anyone else hearing this? trying to figure out if producer gap vs exporter normalization.

Marcus Lin: yep, SUP-18421 is in my inbox. need to triage quickly: (1) are events no...
text:
Hanae Suzuki: seeing a couple pings from Support + SE: Private customers not seeing RBAC change events (role updates, user added to project). Anyone else hearing this? trying to figure out if producer gap vs exporter normalization.

Marcus Lin: yep, SUP-18421 is in my inbox. need to triage quickly: (1) are events not emitted, or (2) emitted but dropped in private pipeline, or (3) emitted but schema mismatch so exporter filters.

Vivek Kulkarni: FWIW I’ve got Helio Bank asking for “proof of coverage” for Private parity. If RBAC is missing that’s a blocker. What’s the fastest way to confirm across a couple private envs?

Kevin Osei: exporter side: we *do* see audit payloads arriving, but field sets inconsistent and some get rejected by validator when missing `event_type` / `actor` shape. If older private services are still on legacy shape, exporter normalization may not be catching.

Se...

title: #eng-security: 1737021142
preview:
Marcus Lin: kicking off quick thread on audit event schema v2. we need to land field list + category taxonomy this week for the exporter workstream. main open q: where do we draw the line on `source_ip` + `user_agent` given privacy/PII.

Naomi Feldman: +1. PRD calls out “source IP / user agent where applicable” but ...
text:
Marcus Lin: kicking off quick thread on audit event schema v2. we need to land field list + category taxonomy this week for the exporter workstream. main open q: where do we draw the line on `source_ip` + `user_agent` given privacy/PII.

Naomi Feldman: +1. PRD calls out “source IP / user agent where applicable” but we need a crisp definition so customers don’t assume we log everything.

Kevin Osei: current v1 is inconsistent anyway. some events have IP in structured logs, but audit payload doesn’t. for SIEM mapping, security teams *do* ask for IP + UA to correlate suspicious logins.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: from RBAC side, we also need actor + auth method. categories matter for filtering (admins will want “auth” vs “deployment changes” etc).

Marcus Lin: propose we standardize around:
- `event_id` (stable, unique)
- `occurred_at` (when action happened)
- `emitted_at` (when we wrote audit reco...

title: #eng-security: 1736964821
preview:
Marcus Lin: Dropping schema v1 proposal for review. Main open questions before we lock ADR: required fields (actor / impersonation / source_ip) + event_id/idempotency expectations. Doc draft here: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SEC/audit-log-schema-v1 (WIP)

Allison Grant: skimming now. +1 on keepin...
text:
Marcus Lin: Dropping schema v1 proposal for review. Main open questions before we lock ADR: required fields (actor / impersonation / source_ip) + event_id/idempotency expectations. Doc draft here: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SEC/audit-log-schema-v1 (WIP)

Allison Grant: skimming now. +1 on keeping top-level stable + namespaced extensions. but we need to be crisp on what’s REQUIRED vs “best effort”. SIEM folks will assume missing fields == broken producer.

Kevin Osei: exporter perspective: duplicates will happen (retry + buffering). need a durable `event_id` that can be used as de-dupe key downstream. if we don’t guarantee uniqueness, customers will ask us how to de-dupe and we’ll have no story.

Hanae Suzuki: on required fields, can we align with: actor, action, target, tenant, time, result. everything else optional? source_ip is useful but not always present (pri...

title: #eng-security: 1734128045
preview:
Priya Natarajan: quick q on audit log export requests are we allowing cross-region sinks (e.g., us-east hosted exporting to eu-west bucket) if customer asks?

Connor OBrien: we *shouldnt* by default. it breaks residency expectations + makes troubleshooting worse.

Evelyn Hart: +1. also connector in us-east assuming ...
text:
Priya Natarajan: quick q on audit log export requests are we allowing cross-region sinks (e.g., us-east hosted exporting to eu-west bucket) if customer asks?

Connor OBrien: we *shouldnt* by default. it breaks residency expectations + makes troubleshooting worse.

Evelyn Hart: +1. also connector in us-east assuming role into eu-west bucket has weird IAM + endpoint behavior.

Vanessa Ortiz: policy: default no cross-region. if customer insists, we need Legal + Security signoff + explicit customer ack in the ticket.

Priya Natarajan: ok. what about multi-region customers w/ separate prefixes per region?

Vanessa Ortiz: thats fine. keep export regional; separate prefixes (or buckets) per region.

Connor OBrien: also note: for Azure, cross-region storage accounts can be global-ish, but still treat as residency-impacting unless Legal clears.

Priya Natarajan: got it. Ill add to controls pag...

title: #eng-security: 1718888888
preview:
Maya Patel: Heads-up — we tightened keystore role scope in ap-southeast-1 this morning and I'm seeing a spike in mTLS handshake failures from proxy -> model-serving (~10% errors). Not sure if RBAC pruning cut off decrypt for a subset of replicas. Anyone seeing the same?
Arun Singh: saw the alert. noticed errors arou...
text:
Maya Patel: Heads-up — we tightened keystore role scope in ap-southeast-1 this morning and I'm seeing a spike in mTLS handshake failures from proxy -> model-serving (~10% errors). Not sure if RBAC pruning cut off decrypt for a subset of replicas. Anyone seeing the same?
Arun Singh: saw the alert. noticed errors around 08:22 UTC. stack traces show KMS unwrap denies.
Priya Chen: thanks for flagging. can you paste the deny logs? KMS or proxy?
Maya Patel: logs: ```ERROR proxy: mTLS handshake fail: tls: failed to verify client certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority; upstream unwrap error: AccessDenied: Permission denied for kms:Decrypt for key arn:aws:kms:ap-southeast-1:123456:key/abcd-efgh```
Ben Carter: that looks like IAM/KMS permission scope change. the role used by keystore-service was narrowed to granular arn/* earlier in the rollout. maybe we removed kms:Decrypt ...

title: #eng-security: 1712345678
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — we're planning to roll out audit schema v3 to staging today. Major goals: clearer principal fields, deterministic resource identifiers for downstream SIEMs, and an opt-in customer validation API.
Ravi Patel: +1. Can you list the breaking changes so Support/docs can prep comms?
Ava Chen: quick li...
text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — we're planning to roll out audit schema v3 to staging today. Major goals: clearer principal fields, deterministic resource identifiers for downstream SIEMs, and an opt-in customer validation API.
Ravi Patel: +1. Can you list the breaking changes so Support/docs can prep comms?
Ava Chen: quick list: `actor_id` -> `principal_id` (kept as string), `resource` object shape flattened to `target.type` and `target.id`, `timestamp` now always RFC3339 with timezone, removed `legacy_action` (deprecated). Also added `event_fingerprint` for dedupe.
Samir Khan: Are we changing any RBAC event names? e.g., `role.grant` -> `rbac.grant`? Need to update our detection rules.
Ava Chen: good catch — yes: top-level `action` values were namespaced. Old `role.grant` stays in deprecated list for 30 days, but new canonical is `rbac.grant`. We'll include mapping in changelog.
Elena Gomez: KM...

title: #eng-security: 1712105555
preview: Olivia Chen: Auditor requested a combined evidence package covering RBAC changes, KMS usage, vuln triage notes, and any incident links for the 24h window on 2025-01-10. They want chain-of-custody for each RBAC change + KMS decrypt traces and a short mapping to any vulnerabilty patches that touched affected services....
text:
Olivia Chen: Auditor requested a combined evidence package covering RBAC changes, KMS usage, vuln triage notes, and any incident links for the 24h window on 2025-01-10. They want chain-of-custody for each RBAC change + KMS decrypt traces and a short mapping to any vulnerabilty patches that touched affected services. Thoughts on scope and redaction?
Samir Patel: I'd keep the scope tight: RBAC change events, SCIM provisioning deltas, k8s RoleBinding/ClusterRoleBinding changes, CloudTrail KMS Decrypt/GenerateDataKey events, and a small CSV mapping changes -> PR/CHG ids. Redact PII, keep hashed principals.
Maria Gomez: Agree. For vuln triage, we can include vulnerability tickets (internal IDs) and the fix deployment timestamp. Do not export customer data. We'll provide CVE or internal vuln id but scrub stack traces.
Priya Rao: For incident linkage, include only incidents that had a direct...

title: #eng-security: 1712000000
preview: Olivia Chen: Hi team — external auditor asked for a short sample to validate RBAC changes and accompanying KMS access proofs for a 24h window last month. They want: (1) list of RBAC modifications (who/what/when), (2) signed KMS decrypt/log entries showing key usage, and (3) a checksumed bundle we can hand off. How d...
text:
Olivia Chen: Hi team — external auditor asked for a short sample to validate RBAC changes and accompanying KMS access proofs for a 24h window last month. They want: (1) list of RBAC modifications (who/what/when), (2) signed KMS decrypt/log entries showing key usage, and (3) a checksumed bundle we can hand off. How do we best produce this without leaking unrelated PII?
Samir Patel: Sounds familiar. We usually run a filtered export: RBAC change events from IAM audit logs + Splunk query for KMS CloudTrail events, then package with a manifest and SHA256 of each file.
Maria Gomez: +1. For RBAC diffs I can pull k8s rolebinding change events and our SCIM provisioning logs. Will filter by org_id and time range.
AuditorBot: Please include the change request tickets or PR refs for each change if possible. Auditor needs mapping: change -> approval -> deployment.
Olivia Chen: Good call, include P...

title: #eng-security: 1711067890
preview:
Maya Singh: flagging a permissions bug — I just saw a junior engineer trigger a canary rollout from the UI but they don't have rollout rights according to our role matrix.
Ethan Park: yikes. which user and when?
Maya Singh: user=julia.kim, ts=1711067845, deploy-service logs show grant: true for route /rollouts/start...
text:
Maya Singh: flagging a permissions bug — I just saw a junior engineer trigger a canary rollout from the UI but they don't have rollout rights according to our role matrix.
Ethan Park: yikes. which user and when?
Maya Singh: user=julia.kim, ts=1711067845, deploy-service logs show grant: true for route /rollouts/start even though julia only has deploy.read and deploy.execute.
Jordan Lee: looking at last week's RBAC refactor PR (#502), mapping table changed: ops.rollouts -> rollout.manage, but some middleware still expects ops.rollouts. possible mismatch.
Sanjay Rao: quick grep in deploy-service: enforcement uses `required = config.get("required_perm")` and later `if required in user.perms: allow` — when required_perm is missing the default is empty string which empircally returned true in our legacy check.
Maya Singh: also noticed KMS audit stream has a gap — decrypt events for rollout-...
